taxonomy: Sabulina graminifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. It belongs to the genus Sabulina, which is part of the family Aizoaceae, commonly known as the ice plant family. The species Sabulina graminifolia is native to South Africa. The plant is a succulent, which is characterized by its ability to store water in its leaves, stems, or roots. This adaptation helps it survive in arid environments.
